checks.question: Does the final diff directly resolve the critique and review flags that were raised?
checks.guidance: Only mark a flag resolved when the final diff or explicit verification evidence shows the concern is addressed in code or committed artifacts.

findings.detail: `useRenderBudget` was implemented with DEV-gated runtime logic, 1s idle reset, over-budget warning behavior, and the settled `max` aggregation semantics.
`RenderTelemetryOverlay` is mounted from `App.tsx`, uses a non-conflicting shortcut, and the settled color/status rule is derived from the same `max` aggregate.
The concrete timeline row target was addressed via `TrackListRenderer.tsx` / `SortableRow`, satisfying the earlier ambiguity about the single-row surface.
The baseline artifact is still incomplete: the recorded baselines cover only three components and explicitly say the `>=5 components x >=3 interactions` bar was not met.

The non-HMR dev-built measurement path remains unresolved: the baseline notes state `build:dev + preview` was unusable for DEV-only telemetry because `import.meta.env.DEV` was false in the previewed build.
Full validation is still red because `npm test` did not pass in the final verification evidence.

checks.question: Do all hard-gate success criteria pass based on the final implementation and verification evidence?
checks.guidance: Any failed `must` criterion requires `needs_rework`. Trust executor evidence unless the diff/audit makes the claim ambiguous.

findings.detail: Hook implementation criterion passes based on the recorded file inspection and reviewer verdict for `useRenderBudget`.
Overlay criterion passes based on the recorded file inspection and reviewer verdict for `RenderTelemetryOverlay` and `App.tsx`.
Instrumentation coverage was accepted from executor evidence, including `SortableRow` and inner wrapper placement for `TasksPane`/`PreviewPanel`.
Baseline documentation/coverage criterion fails because the captured dataset does not meet the required coverage threshold.
Budget calibration criterion fails because most required surfaces were never measured in the required baseline flow, so the budgets were not validated against the milestone baseline.
Validation criterion fails because `npm test` remained red, even though typecheck, build, bundle grep, and synthetic smoke passed.

pre_check_flags.detail: Diff size looks larger than expected: changed_lines=157, expected≈10, ratio=15.70, files=10, hunks=16.

criteria.name: `useRenderBudget(name, budget): void` is implemented in `src/shared/dev/useRenderBudget.ts` with React hooks declared unconditionally, dev-gated runtime telemetry logic, 1s idle reset, and over-budget warning behavior.
criteria.evidence: Pass. T1 and SC1 both record direct inspection of [useRenderBudget.ts](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/dev/useRenderBudget.ts) and confirm unconditional hooks, DEV-gated runtime logic, the 1s idle reset, first-over-budget warning behavior, and the settled `max` aggregation semantics.

criteria.name: `RenderTelemetryOverlay` is mounted only in dev, hidden by default, persisted via `localStorage`, uses a non-conflicting keybind, and shows live per-name count/budget status.
criteria.evidence: Pass. T2 and SC2 confirm the overlay is DEV-only, mounted from [App.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/app/App.tsx), default-hidden, persisted in `localStorage`, and bound to `Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Y` instead of the conflicting `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R`; the settled red/green status rule is tied to the same `max` aggregate in [RenderTelemetryOverlay.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/dev/RenderTelemetryOverlay.tsx).

criteria.name: All required surfaces are instrumented, including `TrackListRenderer.tsx` (`SortableRow`), with hook placement in executable inner render functions for wrapped components.
criteria.evidence: Pass. T3 and SC3 explicitly confirm instrumentation at the required executable render boundaries, including [TrackListRenderer.tsx](/workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TrackListRenderer.tsx), plus inner wrapper placement for `TasksPaneComponent` and `PreviewPanelComponent`. The diff/audit is sparse for some files, but the recorded `rg` verification was specific and accepted by the prior reviewer.

criteria.name: Baseline doc is created at `docs/state_management_refactor_baselines.md` with capture date, commit SHA, and measurements covering at least 5 components with at least 3 interactions per measured component.
criteria.evidence: Fail. [state_management_refactor_baselines.md](/workspace/reigh-app/docs/state_management_refactor_baselines.md) exists, but T4/T5/SC4/SC5 all state the recorded baseline covers only three components (`GenerationsPaneGallery`, `TasksPane`, `ShotsPanelContent`) and explicitly does not meet the required `>=5 components x >=3 interactions` coverage bar.

criteria.name: Budgets are calibrated to baseline behavior (raised to observed max + 1 where needed) so normal baseline interactions do not produce persistent warnings.
criteria.evidence: Fail. The recorded measurements were incomplete, so the budgets were only checked against the three observed components. T4 states no increases were required for those three, but the remaining required surfaces were not observed, so milestone-wide calibration was not completed.

criteria.name: `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` succeed after telemetry changes.
criteria.evidence: Fail. T7 records `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run build` passing, but `npm test` remained red with full-suite timeouts in `src/app/main.test.tsx`, `src/integrations/supabase/__tests__/clientContracts.test.ts`, and `src/moduleImportCoverage.test.ts`. `baseline_test_failures` is `null`, so there is no recorded pre-existing baseline to waive this gate.

criteria.name: Production runtime bundles (JS/CSS) in `dist/` contain no `RenderBudget` or `RenderTelemetryOverlay` strings when grepped excluding sourcemaps.
criteria.evidence: Pass. T7 records `npm run build` success and a grep across runtime `dist` assets excluding `.map` files that found no `RenderBudget` or `RenderTelemetryOverlay` strings. This also addresses the earlier sourcemap brittleness concern.

criteria.name: Implementation stays within approved paths (`src/shared/dev/`, `src/app/App.tsx`, `docs/`, and explicitly instrumented component files) with no render-refactor/state-management changes.
criteria.evidence: Pass with caveat. T6 says the telemetry implementation stayed within approved product-code scope, and no render/state-management refactor was introduced. The working tree also contains unrelated changes in `.gitignore`, `.megaplan/...`, and three test files per `execution_audit.json`, but the telemetry work itself remained scoped.

criteria.name: Manual dev-mode smoke verifies toggle, persistence, live counts, and synthetic over-budget warning before handoff.
criteria.evidence: Waived. Manual/runtime smoke cannot be independently replayed in this review, but T7 contains acceptable recorded evidence from the temporary `vite-node` repro script covering toggle, persistence, live updates, over-budget warning, and idle reset.

issues: Baseline coverage is incomplete: the committed baseline doc does not meet the required `>=5 components x >=3 interactions` measurement bar.
The non-HMR dev-built measurement path is still unresolved because the recorded `build:dev + preview` flow did not expose DEV-only telemetry.
The hard validation gate is still red because `npm test` did not pass in the final verification evidence.

rework_items.issue: Baseline capture does not satisfy the milestone coverage requirement.
rework_items.expected: Capture and document baseline counts for at least 5 instrumented components with at least 3 interactions per measured component, using the approved non-HMR measurement flow.
rework_items.actual: The baseline doc records only three observed components and explicitly says the `>=5 components x >=3 interactions` requirement was not met.

rework_items.issue: The planned dev-built measurement path was not actually made usable for DEV-only telemetry.
rework_items.expected: Committed code and measurement flow should allow baseline capture from a non-HMR dev-built artifact so DEV-only telemetry is present during previewed measurement runs.
rework_items.actual: The baseline notes state `build:dev + preview` was unusable because `import.meta.env.DEV` was false in the previewed build, so the required measurement path remained unsolved.

rework_items.issue: Final hard-gate validation is still failing.
rework_items.expected: `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` should all succeed, or pre-existing failures should be explicitly baselined so they are not attributed to this work.
rework_items.actual: `npm test` remained red in final verification with full-suite timeouts; no baseline failure list was provided to exempt those failures.

summary: Needs rework. The telemetry hook, overlay, settled `max` aggregation semantics, instrumentation, and production bundle stripping all have sufficient evidence, but two hard milestone requirements remain unmet: the baseline artifact does not achieve the required measurement coverage and the non-HMR dev-built capture path is still not working, and the full `npm test` gate is still red.

task_verdicts.reviewer_verdict: Pass. The recorded inspection of `useRenderBudget.ts` is specific enough to accept the hook implementation and the settled `max` aggregation contract.
Pass. The overlay implementation was sufficiently verified as DEV-only, persisted, root-mounted, and bound to a non-conflicting shortcut.
Pass. Instrumentation coverage was accepted from explicit `rg` evidence, including the concrete `SortableRow` row surface and wrapped inner components.
Fail. Baseline capture is explicitly incomplete and does not meet the required coverage bar.
Fail. The baseline document exists but cannot satisfy the milestone while the measurement dataset remains incomplete and the dev-built capture path is unresolved.
Pass with caveat. The telemetry implementation stayed within approved product-code scope, though the working tree also contains unrelated non-scope changes.
Fail. Typecheck/build/production-strip verification passed, but the full `npm test` gate did not.

sense_check_verdicts.verdict: Confirmed. The hook behavior and settled aggregation semantics were explicitly verified in `useRenderBudget.ts`.
Confirmed. The overlay is treated as DEV-only, persisted, and bound to a non-conflicting shortcut.
Confirmed. Required instrumentation points, including `SortableRow` and the inner wrapper components, were explicitly accepted.
Not confirmed. The required `>=5 components x >=3 interactions` non-HMR baseline capture did not happen.
Not confirmed. The doc includes metadata and aggregation semantics but still fails the required coverage bar.
Confirmed with caveat. Telemetry code stayed within approved scope, but the working tree includes unrelated extra changes.
Not confirmed. Full end-to-end validation is blocked by the still-failing `npm test` gate.
